title: "Privacy Law Roundup — July 18, 2026"
date: 2026-07-18 10:37:52 +0530
categories: [news, roundup]
tags: [dpdpa, india, privacy, global, news]
---

## India: Regulatory & Compliance Updates

Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed that the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules are now finalized and expected to be published in the coming days, marking a crucial step towards full implementation of the DPDPA. This follows months of industry consultations and preparation. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxQakx6Y1RYS0lCdGEzN1VfZENmenhLRVZRWUViVUNwZHRCZzhZbDJFYkN4UUdOb1NKZzFKUHdIQnRxWDVINnhTaWY5Ml9IUFppTW44ZkVZeTFzTndwaUo2bl9GYmpHU3U4dkV5UEtGbHdXbG93OGs4QXBSQ3I5Ums5SDFGREdYRHdJQXp2YkpzcTVHaC0wLV9qaDlRRnp2Y25obXhIQVdjSHhsbjZFa0wwTA))

MeitY has initiated the process to establish the Data Protection Board of India, inviting applications for the chairperson and members of the new adjudicatory body. This move is vital for operationalizing the DPDPA's enforcement mechanisms and resolving data protection disputes. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiyAFBVV95cUxOaVR4MW9NWUdLSkM5eEVJNFlNeWJBVVRRNzhaU3d0VGdlMlh0V0ExNlNrU2lUdllVMGpYdFFGQ3lIZ1kyOEFIbXNlWks2cHFSX2lTRlB4d1gxY1NDbU1FV3NfRFVYYTB2V29haVpVTEYyWmtDeHFHLURaYnZyaUtlTlptazN3dTc0cVVIajJoenhPTEVCRTFLZjlfVW94OU1YT0U5RWV2VjdZNnJULUlqd1ViM2RBQm02Ti1GTVpWc1hPNnUwZk5XdA))

MeitY is also reportedly seeking industry input on potentially shortening the compliance timelines for the DPDPA, a development that could accelerate the urgency for businesses to adapt. This suggests an ongoing dynamic approach to the Act's rollout and implementation. ([Source](https://news.google.))

As the DPDPA compliance deadline approaches, many Indian businesses, including MSMEs, are reportedly still unprepared, highlighting the urgent need for comprehensive strategies to secure data at the source and navigate new requirements. Experts emphasize that compliance is not exclusive to large enterprises and requires immediate attention across all business sizes. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiyAFBVV95cUxOaTBmdVN1SUJZZUVza2ZZLVBYWGlVWWowcTVJek1XS1BEMlJqVjl5Tzg4YTJtek9aTGFDTHY2ZXR0dzlyVDNzRTIyZkJtUGZUZFJOVk1oQm9XQXNtMDBxQkQxWFlINnBTcTBBdklmVzh4SGhjRGRBUmxvMEtBcnVzNXhBdjFza0FIYjZNN0ZmaG5jcm54aVhMY0Vldzl3ZmI1WVJFTlJJbVJlaTBRMGNqbDlmV0VqYjI4U1JYcmsxYWpVTTFFdDJlSg), [Source](https://news.google.))

## India: Legal Challenges & Interpretations

The Supreme Court of India has issued notice on a constitutional challenge to both the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and the newly finalized Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025. This significant development could impact the Act's implementation timelines and legal certainty for businesses. ([Source](https://news.google.), [Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxNUXo3aUxwM1dVSWIzOUg4dzgtSkEtS210VFhhTkVIeS1KSHU4cHI0Vkd1enlUd0JKTmxHbTlpbDJyWVhPQmwzRWlpZnNXemZUcXRnOG51X0RYbGhYMElfejVJa2t4ZXhidnJEUkJaZVFLam50RkJubWp3VTJ2Q0YwSXAxVHk1UlhBSUsyVjdudk9RTEFraFhXT0M4UU5KNzZE))

Legal analysis suggests that the DPDPA's omission of a statutory definition
